title: stylidium piliferum var. bicolor
intro: Stylidium piliferum var. bicolor, also known as the hairy triggerplant, is a variety of flowering plant that belongs to the Stylidium genus, native to Australia. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial effects. The hairy triggerplant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving skin conditions, and supporting the immune system. It is often prepared as a tincture, tea, or infusion, and its extracts are also used in topical applications for their antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
